engineIntelligence.h2: Seat Leon Engines- Codes, Common Failures& Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Seat Leon in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. All prices reflect supply-only ranges from our specialist network.

cards.altText: Seat Leon CBZB engine- petrol 1.2L replacement UK
productionHistory: Leon Mk2(1P) Reference, SE, Style, FR- 2009-2012
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure- premature wear causes rattling noise and potential engine damage from 60k miles.
Oil consumption increase- piston ring wear on high-mileage units leads to excessive oil use.

cards.altText: Seat Leon CAYC engine- diesel 1.6L replacement UK
productionHistory: Leon Mk2(1P) Reference, SE, Style, FR- 2009-2012
Leon Mk3(5F) Reference, SE, Style, XCELLENCE- 2013-2017
knownFailures: Injector failure- high-pressure injectors degrade causing rough running and misfires after 80k miles.
EGR cooler cracking- known fault leading to coolant loss and potential head gasket damage.

cards.altText: Seat Leon DKRF engine- petrol 1.0L replacement UK
productionHistory: Leon Mk4(KL1/KL8) Reference, SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR- 2017-present
knownFailures: Intake manifold carbon buildup- causes hesitation and reduced performance on direct-injection units.
High-pressure fuel pump wear- may cause hard starting or reduced power on higher-mileage examples.

engineGuide.h2: Seat Leon Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications& Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Seat Leon across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The CAYC 1.6 TDI powered most diesel Leon Mk2/Mk3 models, offering strong economy but becoming notorious for injector and EGR cooler failures at higher mileage.
commonFailures: High-pressure injector wear causing rough running and misfires from 80k miles
EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss and potential head gasket damage
Dual-mass flywheel wear on manual transmission models causing rattling noise
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAYC Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The CRLB 2.0 TDI replaced earlier 2.0 TDI codes for Leon Mk3/Mk4, offering improved emissions compliance and higher power outputs for SE/FR/XCELLENCE trims.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues on short-journey cars leading to clogging and limp mode
Turbo actuator wear causing boost pressure faults and reduced power
High-pressure fuel pump failures on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CRLB Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The CRBC 2.0 TDI was the earlier-generation diesel for Leon Mk2/Mk3, offering strong torque but sharing common EA189 failure patterns with CAYC units.
commonFailures: Injector wear patterns identical to CAYC but on larger displacement
EGR valve sticking causing reduced power and increased emissions
Timing belt service critical at 120k miles to prevent catastrophic failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for CRBC Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel(184 PS)
entries.history: The CUPA 2.0 TDI 184 PS was the high-output diesel for Leon FR models, delivering strong performance but requiring careful maintenance due to higher stress levels.
commonFailures: Turbocharger wear accelerated by higher boost pressures
DPF and EGR issues compounded by performance-oriented driving patterns
Dual-mass flywheel wear more pronounced due to higher torque output
entries.cta: Get quotes for CUPA Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The CBZB 1.2 TSI was the most common petrol engine in Leon Mk2/Mk3, but its EA111 timing chain tensioner design flaw made it the single biggest driver of replacement enquiries.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling noise and potential valve damage from 60k miles
Piston ring wear leading to excessive oil consumption on early-production units
High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBZB Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The CBZC is a variant of the CBZB 1.2 TSI with minor calibration differences, sharing the same timing chain vulnerability and replacement considerations.
commonFailures: Identical timing chain tensioner failure pattern to CBZB
Oil consumption issues on early-production units
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBZC Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The CZEA 1.4 TSI powered Leon Mk3 FR and Cupra models, delivering strong performance but requiring careful maintenance due to turbocharged direct-injection complexity.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner issues on early EA111 1.4 TSI units
Carbon buildup on intake valves requiring periodic walnut blasting
Turbo actuator wear causing boost pressure faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for CZEA Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The CZDA is a closely related variant of the CZEA 1.4 TSI with minor output differences, sharing the same performance characteristics and failure patterns.
commonFailures: Same timing chain and carbon buildup concerns as CZEA
High-pressure fuel pump wear on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CZDA Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The DKRF 1.0 TSI replaced the 1.2 TSI for Leon Mk4, offering improved refinement and efficiency with three-cylinder design- though direct injection requires intake maintenance.
commonFailures: Intake manifold carbon buildup causing hesitation and misfires on direct-injection units
High-pressure fuel pump wear leading to hard starting or reduced power
Timing chain stretch on very high-mileage examples(less common than EA111)
entries.cta: Get quotes for DKRF Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The DKLA is the lower-output variant of the 1.0 TSI family, sharing the same EA211 architecture and maintenance requirements as the DKRF.
commonFailures: Intake carbon buildup pattern identical to DKRF
HPFP wear on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DKLA Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The CHZJ is another variant within the EA211 1.0 TSI family, with calibration differences but shared architecture and failure patterns.
commonFailures: Same intake manifold carbon concerns as other EA211 1.0 TSI codes
Occasional turbo actuator faults on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CHZJ Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The DADA 1.5 TSI Evo arrived with the Leon Mk4 facelift as the new performance petrol option, featuring cylinder deactivation for improved efficiency without sacrificing power.
commonFailures: Early units may experience intake manifold flap motor faults
Carbon buildup remains a consideration for direct-injection design
Turbo-related issues less common but possible on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for DADA Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol(Cupra)
entries.history: The CCZB 2.0 TSI powered Leon Cupra and Cupra R models, delivering high performance but requiring specialist knowledge due to turbocharged complexity and timing chain risks.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure on EA888 Gen 3 units causing catastrophic damage
Turbocharger wear accelerated by high-boost performance driving
High-pressure fuel pump and injector issues on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CCZB Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol(Cupra 280/290)
entries.history: The CDLA 2.0 TSI powered Leon Cupra 280/290 models, representing the peak of EA888 Gen 3 performance with improved reliability over earlier CCZB units.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch less common than CCZB but still requires monitoring
Turbo actuator and wastegate wear on high-mileage performance units
Carbon buildup on intake valves requiring periodic cleaning
entries.cta: Get quotes for CDLA Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol(Cupra 300)
entries.history: The DFGA 2.0 TSI Evo powered the final Leon Cupra 300, featuring updated EA888 Gen 3B architecture with improved efficiency and reliability.
commonFailures: Generally more reliable than earlier EA888 units but still requires maintenance
Turbo and HPFP wear possible on heavily driven examples
Carbon buildup remains a consideration for direct-injection design
entries.cta: Get quotes for DFGA Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol(Cupra 300/310)
entries.history: The DNUE 2.0 TSI Evo represents the latest EA888 Gen 3B iteration for Leon Cupra, offering strong performance with improved long-term reliability.
commonFailures: Early units generally reliable with proper maintenance
Turbo and HPFP wear possible on high-mileage performance driving
Carbon buildup on intake valves requires periodic attention
entries.cta: Get quotes for DNUE Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The CGPA 1.0 MPI provided a simple, naturally aspirated entry-level petrol option for early Leon Mk4 models, prioritising affordability over performance.
commonFailures: Generally robust with fewer catastrophic failure patterns than turbocharged units
Occasional ignition coil or sensor faults on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CGPA Seat Leon engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The BXW 1.4 MPI was a carry-over naturally aspirated engine for early Leon Mk2 models, offering simple reliability but lower performance versus TSI options.
commonFailures: Generally reliable with minimal major failure patterns documented
Occasional thermostat or water pump leaks on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for BXW Seat Leon engine replacement

Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Seat Leon engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Seat Leon Engine Problems- What It Costs to Fix& When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Seat Leon's most common replacement enquiries centre on the 1.2 TSI(CBZB/CBZC) timing chain, 1.6 TDI(CAYC) injector/EGR failures, and 1.0 TSI(DKRF) intake manifold issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point- plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.group: Seat Leon 1.2 TSI Timing Chain Failure- Why CBZB/CBZC Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.h4: Seat Leon 1.2 TSI Timing Chain Failure- Why CBZB/CBZC Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Seat Leon Reference, SE, Style, FR(2009-2017, CBZB& CBZC 1.2 TSI)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that damages guides and can jump timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head work if valves bent; new chain kit included.
repairOptions.longevity: Only if damage limited; often not cost-effective on older Leons.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply& fit rebuilt CBZB/CBZC with upgraded tensioner, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; restores reliability long-term.

problems.recommendation: If your Leon is under 70k miles and showing early chain rattle, a preventative kit from a specialist offers the best value. Once the chain has jumped or valves are bent, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is often more economical than extensive repairs- especially on 10+ year old models.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CBZB engine prices for your Seat Leon

problems.group: Seat Leon 1.6 TDI Injector& EGR Failure- CAYC Diesel Replacement Guide
problems.h4: Seat Leon 1.6 TDI Injector& EGR Failure- CAYC Diesel Replacement Guide
problems.affectedModels: Seat Leon Reference, SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR(2009-2017, CAYC 1.6 TDI)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injectors wear causing poor atomisation; EGR cooler cracks from thermal stress leading to coolant loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, test and refurbish injectors; EGR blanking if applicable.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may delay but not prevent eventual replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injectors and/or EGR cooler with OEM-spec parts.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; labour-intensive on transverse TDI.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply& fit rebuilt CAYC with upgraded injectors, EGR delete option, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution for high-mileage diesel Leons.

problems.recommendation: For Leons over 100k miles with multiple diesel faults, investing in a rebuilt CAYC engine often makes more financial sense than piecemeal repairs. The upgraded internals in a quality rebuild address the root causes of injector and EGR failure.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CAYC Seat Leon diesel engine replacement

problems.group: Seat Leon 1.0 TSI Intake Manifold Issues- DKRF/DKLA Carbon Buildup
problems.h4: Seat Leon 1.0 TSI Intake Manifold Issues- DKRF/DKLA Carbon Buildup
problems.affectedModels: Seat Leon SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR(2017-present, DKRF/DKLA 1.0 TSI)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design allows carbon deposits to accumulate on intake valves, restricting airflow and causing misfires.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold and blast valves clean; no parts replacement.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective maintenance; recommended every 60k miles for direct-injection TSIs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty intake manifold assembly with updated part number.
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses flap motor failures; does not prevent future carbon buildup.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply& fit rebuilt 1.0 TSI with cleaned/updated manifold, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if engine has additional issues like turbo or HPFP wear.

problems.recommendation: Regular walnut blasting every 50-60k miles is the most cost-effective way to maintain a 1.0 TSI Leon. If the engine has suffered misfire-related damage or has high mileage alongside manifold issues, a rebuilt unit offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DKRF engine prices for your Seat Leon

problems.group: Seat Leon Oil Consumption- Early TSI Piston Ring Wear(CBZB/CBZC)
problems.h4: Seat Leon Oil Consumption- Early TSI Piston Ring Wear(CBZB/CBZC)
problems.affectedModels: Seat Leon Reference, SE, Style(2009-2015, CBZB/CBZC 1.2 TSI)
problems.rootCause: Early EA111 1.2 TSI engines had piston ring design that allowed excessive oil passage into combustion chamber.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Regular oil level checks and top-ups between services.
repairOptions.longevity: Only delays inevitable; not a repair, just damage limitation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine out, strip bottom end, fit updated piston rings and seals.
repairOptions.longevity: Labour-intensive; often approaches cost of rebuilt unit on older Leons.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply& fit rebuilt engine with updated piston design, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Most economical permanent solution; eliminates oil consumption issue entirely.

problems.recommendation: If your 1.2 TSI Leon is burning over 1 litre per 1,000 miles and is over 70k miles, a rebuilt engine with updated pistons is almost always better value than an in-situ ring replacement. The warranty coverage also provides peace of mind that top-ups cannot offer.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned CBZB Seat Leon engine with warranty

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Seat Leon
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with timing chain rattle on a 1.2 TSI, injector failure on a 1.6 TDI, or carbon buildup on a 1.0 TSI, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Seat Leon specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Seat Leon Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Seat Leon and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty- always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Seat Leon Engine Types- Options, Costs& What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on your Leon's variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Seat Leon Reconditioned Engines(Recon)
types.description: Professionally rebuilt Seat Leon engines with worn parts replaced, tested to OEM standards and backed by warranty coverage.
types.frontDescription: Professionally rebuilt Seat Leon engines with worn parts replaced, tested to OEM standards and backed by warranty coverage.
types.frontDisclaimer: Prices vary by engine code, mileage and supplier location.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Seat Leon engines undergo strip, clean, inspect and rebuild with new gaskets, seals and worn components replaced. Ideal for owners wanting near-new reliability without new-engine cost.

Bench-tested before dispatch with compression report

types.title: Seat Leon Rebuilt Engines

types.title: Seat Leon Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage, tested second-hand Seat Leon engines sourced from UK breakers- the most budget-friendly replacement option.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage, tested second-hand Seat Leon engines sourced from UK breakers- the most budget-friendly replacement option.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage, warranty and condition vary by supplier; verify before purchase.
types.backDescription: Used Seat Leon engines are removed from written-off or scrapped vehicles, compression-tested and offered with short-term warranty. Best for tight budgets or short-term ownership plans.

engineSizes.h2: Seat Leon Engines by Size- Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Seat Leon across UK generations is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Seat Leon 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 TDI(CAYC) was the backbone of the Leon diesel range(2009-2017), offering an excellent balance of fuel economy and performance- but known for injector and EGR cooler failures at higher mileage.
commonFailurePoints: High-pressure injector wear from 80k miles; EGR cooler cracking causing coolant loss; dual-mass flywheel wear on manual models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Seat Leon 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Seat Leon 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TDI(CRLB/CRBC) powered higher-spec Leon models from 2009-present, delivering strong torque for motorway driving- though complexity means higher replacement costs and DPF-related concerns.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on short-journey cars; turbo actuator wear; high-pressure fuel pump failures on higher-mileage units.
items.cta: Get quotes for Seat Leon 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Seat Leon 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0 TSI(DKRF/DKLA/CHZJ) replaced the 1.2 TSI for Leon Mk4(2017-present), offering improved efficiency and refinement with three-cylinder smoothness- though direct injection requires regular intake cleaning.
commonFailurePoints: Intake manifold carbon buildup on direct-injection units; high-pressure fuel pump wear on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Seat Leon 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat Leon 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2 TSI(CBZB/CBZC) powered most Leon Mk2/Mk3 petrol models(2009-2017) and remains the most common replacement engine due to widespread timing chain tensioner failures on early EA111 units.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling
potential valve damage from 60k miles; piston ring wear leading to oil consumption.
items.cta: Compare Seat Leon 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat Leon 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TSI(CZEA/CZDA) was reserved for Leon FR and Cupra models(2015-2017), delivering strong performance with turbocharged power- though complexity means higher replacement costs.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner issues on early EA111 1.4 TSI; carbon buildup on intake valves; turbo actuator wear.
items.cta: Compare Seat Leon 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat Leon 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5 TSI Evo(DADA) arrived with the Leon Mk4 facelift(2018-present) as the new performance petrol option, featuring cylinder deactivation for improved efficiency without sacrificing power.
commonFailurePoints: Early units may experience intake manifold flap issues; carbon buildup remains a consideration for direct-injection design.
items.cta: Compare Seat Leon 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat Leon 1.8/2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8 TSI and 2.0 TSI(CCXZ/CCZB/CDLA) powered Leon Cupra and FR performance models(2009-2020), delivering high power outputs- though timing chain and turbo issues drive replacement enquiries.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure on EA888 Gen 3 units; turbo actuator wear; high-pressure fuel pump issues.
items.cta: Compare Seat Leon 1.8/2.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Seat Leon Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Seat Leon engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options- from early 1.2 TSI to the latest 1.5 TSI Evo.

items.title: Seat Leon Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel Seat Leon models(primarily 1.6 TDI and 2.0 TDI) were popular for high-mileage drivers but face expensive injector, EGR and DPF failures that often drive replacement decisions on older generation cars.
items.descriptor: Diesel Seat Leon models(primarily 1.6 TDI and 2.0 TDI) were popular for high-mileage drivers but face expensive injector, EGR and DPF failures that often drive replacement decisions on older generation cars.

CRLB- 2.0 TDI(110-150 PS), higher-spec SE/FR/XCELLENCE, DPF& turbo concerns at higher mileage

Leon Mk4(KL1)(2017-present)- DKRF, DKLA, CHZJ, DADA

1.0 TSI direct injection requires regular walnut blasting to prevent carbon issues

items.title: Seat Leon Hybrid/ Electrified Engines

items.descriptor: The Seat Leon has been offered with mild-hybrid(eTSI) and plug-in hybrid(e-HYBRID) powertrains in the UK market from Mk4 onwards; replacement enquiries remain low but specialist knowledge is required.

engineYears.h2: Seat Leon Engines by Year- What Was Fitted& When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Seat Leon production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The third-generation Leon launched with carry-over VW Group engines including the problematic early 1.2 TSI and new 1.6 TDI diesel.
keyChanges: New PQ35 platform debut for Leon Mk2(1P chassis)
Introduction of EA111 1.2 TSI(CBZB/CBZC) petrol engines
New EA189 1.6 TDI(CAYC) diesel replaces older Pumpe-Düse units
Reference, SE, Style, FR trim levels at launch
mainEngines: CBZB- 1.2L petrol TSI(85-105 PS)(Reference, SE, Style, FR)
CAYC- 1.6L diesel TDI(90-105 PS)(Reference, SE, Style, FR)
knownFor: Early CBZB 1.2 TSI timing chain tensioner issues emerge in owner reports
Strong diesel demand for high-mileage drivers
Value-focused positioning drives high used-engine replacement volume
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2009 Seat Leon replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Leon 1.2 TSI engine replacement • CBZB timing chain rattle • 1.6 TDI injector failure

years.description: Engine line-up stabilises with CBZB 1.2 TSI and CAYC 1.6 TDI dominating sales; timing chain concerns grow for early 1.2 TSI units.
keyChanges: Minor trim updates; engine codes unchanged
FR performance variant introduced with 1.4 TSI(CZEA)
Growing owner reports of 1.2 TSI timing chain noise
knownFor: CBZB timing chain tensioner failures become widespread from 60k miles
FR 1.4 TSI attracts performance enthusiasts but commands premium replacement costs
CAYC diesel remains reliable if serviced but injector failures emerge post-80k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Seat Leon replacement engines

years.description: The 5F generation brought updated styling, Euro 6-compliant engines and new 1.0 TSI code introductions alongside carry-over 1.2 TSI units.
keyChanges: New MQB platform debut for Leon Mk3(5F chassis)
Introduction of EA211 1.0 TSI(DKRF) for select markets
CRLB 2.0 TDI added as new diesel option for higher-spec models
XCELLENCE trim introduced as new top-spec variant
knownFor: Timing chain issues persist on carry-over CBZB engines
New CRLB 2.0 TDI shows improved emissions but DPF concerns on short-journey cars
DKRF 1.0 TSI preview hints at future EA211 dominance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2014 Seat Leon replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Leon 5F engine codes • 2.0 TDI DPF problems • 1.2 TSI vs 1.0 TSI reliability

years.description: Final production years for Leon Mk3 see Cupra performance variant launch and growing awareness of 1.2 TSI replacement needs.
keyChanges: Cupra flagship variant launched with 1.8 TSI(CJSB) and later 2.0 TSI(CCZB/CDLA)
Final CBZB 1.2 TSI production; replacement demand peaks for 2009-2012 units
Growing specialist network for rebuilt EA111 engines
knownFor: CBZB replacement market matures with multiple rebuilt suppliers now offering upgraded tensioners
Cupra 2.0 TSI commands premium pricing but strong enthusiast demand
CRLB diesel remains popular for high-mileage replacements if DPF addressed
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2016 Seat Leon replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Leon Cupra engine replacement • CBZB rebuilt with upgraded tensioner • 2.0 TDI DPF rebuild vs replace

years.description: All-new MQB A0 platform brings EA211 1.0 TSI(DKRF/DKLA) as the new mainstream engine, largely retiring the problematic 1.2 TSI.
keyChanges: New MQB A0 platform debut for Leon Mk4(KL1 chassis)
EA211 1.0 TSI(DKRF/DKLA) replaces EA111 1.2 TSI as mainstream petrol
Carry-over CRLB 2.0 TDI for diesel; no new diesel codes at launch
Reference, SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR trim structure retained
knownFor: DKRF 1.0 TSI shows improved refinement but direct injection requires intake maintenance
No timing chain crisis on EA211; replacement demand initially lower than Leon Mk3
CRLB diesel carry-over means familiar DPF/EGR patterns persist for diesel buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017 Seat Leon replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Leon 2017 engine code • DKRF vs CBZB reliability • 1.0 TSI intake cleaning cost

years.description: Engine line-up stabilises with DKRF/DKLA 1.0 TSI dominating; first DADA 1.5 TSI Evo units appear in FR models.
keyChanges: Introduction of DADA 1.5 TSI Evo(150 PS) for FR facelift models
Minor calibration updates to DKRF/DKLA; no major reliability changes
Growing awareness of EA211 intake carbon maintenance needs
mainEngines: DADA- 1.5L petrol TSI Evo(150 PS)(FR late 2018+)
knownFor: DADA 1.5 TSI Evo introduces cylinder deactivation; early units generally reliable
DKRF/DKLA intake carbon buildup becomes documented maintenance item around 60k miles
Replacement market for Leon Mk4 still developing; focus remains on Leon Mk3 CBZB/CAYC
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2019 Seat Leon replacement engines

years.description: Final evolution of Leon Mk4 sees refined EA211 engines, updated trim structure and growing replacement options for early DKRF units now reaching higher mileage.
keyChanges: Minor styling and tech updates for late KL1/KL8 production
No new engine codes; DKRF/DKLA/DADA continue with calibration refinements
First DKRF units from 2017 now reaching 80k+ miles; replacement enquiries begin to rise
knownFor: DKRF/DKLA replacement market begins to develop as early units age
Intake carbon cleaning remains key maintenance item for direct-injection EA211
DADA 1.5 TSI Evo still under warranty for most UK examples; minimal replacement demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2024 Seat Leon replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Leon 2020 engine replacement • DKRF intake cleaning vs rebuild • 1.5 TSI Evo warranty coverage

faq.h2: Seat Leon Engine Replacement- Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Seat Leon?
items.answer: Yes, if your Leon is in good condition otherwise. A rebuilt engine typically costs £750-£2,800 depending on code, often less than the car's value for FR/Cupra or well-maintained SE/XCELLENCE models.
keyPoints: Rebuilt CBZB 1.2 TSI from £750; CAYC 1.6 TDI from £850
FR and Cupra models justify higher replacement investment
Supply& fit available nationwide from £250 additional labour

items.question: What is the most common Seat Leon engine problem?
items.answer: The CBZB/CBZC 1.2 TSI timing chain tensioner failure is the single biggest issue, affecting Leon Mk2/Mk3 models from 2009-2017 and often requiring full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Failure typically occurs between 60,000-90,000 miles
Rattling noise on cold start is the key warning sign
Rebuilt engines with upgraded tensioners prevent recurrence

items.question: How much does a Seat Leon replacement engine cost?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt engine prices range from £750 for a 1.2 TSI to £2,800 for a 1.5 TSI FR unit, with used options available from £400 depending on mileage and warranty.
keyPoints: CBZB 1.2 TSI rebuilt: £750-£1,600 supply only
CAYC 1.6 TDI rebuilt: £850-£2,100 supply only
DKRF 1.0 TSI rebuilt: £950-£2,200 supply only

items.question: What engines fit the Seat Leon?
items.answer: Only engines with matching engine codes, mountings and ECU compatibility should be fitted- use your registration to verify the exact code for your Leon variant and year.
keyPoints: Leon Mk2(1P) uses EA111 codes: CBZB, CAYC, CZEA
Leon Mk3(5F) uses EA111/EA211 codes: CBZB, DKRF, CRLB
Leon Mk4(KL1) uses EA211 codes: DKRF, DKLA, DADA

items.question: Does a rebuilt Seat Leon engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes- every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with some suppliers offering extended terms.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering

items.question: How much does a timing chain replacement cost on a Seat Leon 1.2 TSI?
items.answer: A preventative timing chain kit costs £450-£750 fitted by a specialist, but if the chain has already jumped or damaged valves, a rebuilt CBZB engine(£750-£1,600) is often better value.
keyPoints: Preventative chain kit: £450-£750 fitted if caught early
Valve repair after failure: £1,100-£1,800 often not cost-effective
Rebuilt CBZB with upgraded tensioner: £750-£1,600 with warranty

items.question: What is my Seat Leon engine code and where do I find it?
items.answer: Your engine code is stamped on the engine block(usually near the gearbox flange) and listed on your V5C logbook under "P.5"- common codes include CBZB, CAYC, DKRF.
keyPoints: Check V5C logbook section P.5 for factory engine code
Physical stamp location: engine block near gearbox or on timing cover
Enter your registration online for instant code verification via DVLA

items.question: Can I get a Seat Leon engine fitted near me?
items.answer: Yes- EngineMarket's network includes specialists offering supply& fit services nationwide, with typical fitting costs of £250-£650 depending on engine accessibility and workshop location.
keyPoints: Supply& fit available from vetted UK Seat Leon specialists
Typical fitting cost: £250-£650 added to engine price
Many suppliers offer collection/delivery and courtesy car options

items.question: Are used Seat Leon engines reliable?
items.answer: Used engines can be reliable if sourced from low-mileage, non-accident donors and compression-tested, but carry shorter warranties(3-6 months) versus rebuilt units(12+ months).
keyPoints: Used CBZB/CAYC from £400-£1,200 depending on mileage
Always request compression test report and mileage verification
Rebuilt engines offer longer warranty and upgraded failure-point components

items.question: What's the cheapest way to replace a Seat Leon engine?
items.answer: A used engine from a reputable breaker is the lowest upfront cost(£400-£900), but a rebuilt unit with warranty often provides better long-term value and peace of mind.
keyPoints: Used CBZB/CAYC: £400-£900 supply only, 3-6 month warranty typical
Rebuilt CBZB/CAYC: £750-£2,100 supply only, 12-month warranty standard
Factor in fitting costs(£250-£650) and potential additional repairs

headingLines: Seat Leon Engine Replacement- Frequently Asked Questions
